title: parietaria officinalis subsp. judaica
intro: Parietaria officinalis subsp. judaica, also known as Jerusalem pepper or False cleaver, is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its small, white or greenish flowers and compound leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Parietaria officinalis subsp. judaica include its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, as well as its potential to aid in the treatment of allergies and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made from its leaves and flowers, and can be consumed as teas, infusions, or added to topical creams and ointments.
